name: Cascada de la Cimbarra and charco del Negrillo - circular by the natural cascade of Cimbarra
name_original: Cascada de la Cimbarra y charco del Negrillo - circular por el paraje natural cascada del Cimbarra
region_intro: Hiking around Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra features dramatic landscapes characterized by spectacular waterfalls, steep quartzite relief, and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The area includes prominent waterfalls like Cascada de la Cimbarra and Cascada del Cimbarrillo, where the Guarrizas River carves into ancient rock formations. Trails often traverse small holm oak and cork oak copses, alongside riverbank forests. This geological setting provides varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra The most popular hiking route is Cascada de la Cimbarra and charco del Negrillo - circular by the natural cascade of Cimbarra, an easy 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the main waterfall and the secluded Charco del Negrillo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cimbarrillo Waterfall – Desfiladero Viewpoint loop from Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra, a moderate 1.9 miles (3.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the Cimbarrillo Waterfall and the surrounding canyon from the Desfiladero Viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Desfiladero Viewpoint – Cimbarrillo Waterfall loop from Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through steep relief and Mediterranean scrubland, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Hiking around Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra is defined by spectacular waterfalls, steep quartzite relief, and diverse Mediterranean scrubland. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 112 hikers have used komoot to explore Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Which waterfalls can I expect to see while hiking in Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra?
A: The most prominent is the Cascada de la Cimbarra, a dramatic 40-meter fall where the Guarrizas River plunges over ancient quartzite. You can also discover the Cascada del Cimbarrillo and the more secluded &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The best time to experience their full power is after periods of rain.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views of the area?
A: Yes, the area features several excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking vistas of the canyon and surrounding landscape. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarrillo Waterfall – Desfiladero Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate these scenic spots.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra is a Special Bird Protection Area, so you might spot impressive birds like the golden eagle, Bonelli's eagle, and eagle owl. On the ground, foxes and genets roam, and otters can be found near the rivers. The diverse Mediterranean vegetation supports a rich ecosystem.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981197453/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-by-the-natural-cascade-of-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo circular trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint – Cimbarrillo Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 4.4 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra for hiking?
A: While beautiful year-round, the waterfalls are most spectacular after periods of rain, typically in late autumn, winter, and spring. These seasons also offer milder temperatures for hiking compared to the summer months. Always check local weather conditions before your visit.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore in the area?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, the area boasts significant cultural heritage. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt;, located near the main waterfall. The region is also home to prehistoric rock art sites, some of which are UNESCO World Heritage sites, showcasing ancient human presence.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of the hiking trails?
A: The trails in Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra offer options for both easy and moderate ability levels. While some paths can be steep or slippery, especially after rain, good footwear and caution are generally sufficient. There are no difficult-rated trails in the immediate area.

Q: Is there dedicated parking available for hikers?
A: Yes, there are designated parking areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Parking&lt;/a&gt; is a convenient option. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Martin Perez Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; also provides parking, ensuring a peaceful environment for visitors.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Paraje Natural Cascada de Cimbarra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 25 reviews. Reviewers often praise the dramatic waterfalls, the unique geological formations, and the well-marked trails that make exploring the diverse landscapes enjoyable.

Q: Are there any longer hiking options for experienced hikers?
A: While many routes are shorter, there are longer moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarrillo Waterfall – Desfiladero Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to approximately 14.6 km, offering a more extensive exploration of the park's features and views.

Q: What kind of vegetation can be found along the trails?
A: Hikers will encounter diverse plant life, including small holm oak and cork oak copses, thick Mediterranean scrubland, and lush riverbank forests along the water channels. Reforestation efforts have also introduced stone pines and black pines, adding to the area's natural aromas.

Q: Are the trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails are suitable for families, especially the easier, shorter circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Martin Perez Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt;, also known as 'Del Cimbarrillo,' provides a refreshing stop with facilities, making it a good base for family outings.
tips: Viewpoint with the best panoramic views of the Cimbarra waterfall and the entire natural area. The Cimbarra waterfall, approximately 40 meters high, which when it carries water creates a spectacular waterfall worth photographing.
Charco del Negrillo, one of the most fascinating geological enclaves in the Sierra Morena de Jaén. Although difficult to access in its last 50 meters, but it is worth making an effort.
Cimbarrillo Waterfall, which is made up of three staggered waterfalls from the Martín Pérez Stream.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Cascada de la Cimbarra
